UFV Fall 2026 Timetable

 This project restores UFV's classic timetable format, bringing back the simple, familiar, and practical way to browse class information. It uses data from UFV's Browse Classes system and Reserved Seats system. While efforts are made to keep this document accurate and up to date, information may be incomplete, outdated, or incorrect. Always verify course details using UFV's official Schedule of Classes.

View official schedule of classes here:
https://www.ufv.ca/registration/semester-info/class-schedule/

 View official schedule changes here:
https://www.ufv.ca/arfiles/includes/202609-schedule-changes.htm

 This document was last updated:
June 12, 2026 at 8:00AM

AGRI 373  	ONE HEALTH & AGRICULTURE  3.0
								Course description: https://www.ufv.ca/calendar/current/CourseDescriptions/AGRI.htm#373
								Prerequisite(s): One of AGRI 237, BIO 111, or 60 university-level credits.

								Course description: https://www.ufv.ca/calendar/current/CourseDescriptions/BIO.htm#093

	NOTE: Students missing more than two classes will receive an F grade in the course, this
	      includes the first class.

	                                                                                                               RESERVED SEATS: 0
	Instructional Method: Face-to-Face Learning (TRD)

	      recommended to have an independent level of French proficiency, equivalent to a B1
	      Diplôme d’Etudes en Langue Française (DELF) level. Students who are unsure about
	      their proficiency level are encouraged to contact the course instructor for a
	      consultation.

	NOTE: Department approval required prior to registration.

								Course description: https://www.ufv.ca/calendar/current/CourseDescriptions/ENGR.htm#123
								Prerequisite(s): One of the following: (A or better in one of Calculus 12 or Pre-calculus 12) or

								                 MATH 092 and MATH 093) or (C+ or better in MATH 110) or (at least 70% on the

ENGL 108  	INTRO TO LIT: WORDS & WORLDS  3.0
								Course description: https://www.ufv.ca/calendar/current/CourseDescriptions/ENGL.htm#108
								Prerequisite(s): One of the following: (B or better in English 12 or English Literature 12), (C
								                 or better in CMNS 099 or ENGL 099), (C+ or better in ENGL 081, ENGL 091, ESL
								                 WG84, or EAP 084), (C- or better in any university-level English or
								                 Communications course), CPT score of 48 or better, LPI minimum score of 30/40
								                 or level 5 in the essay section, or (Test of English as a Foreign Language
								                 (TOEFL) score of 570 or better (or 230 if computer-based score) plus a minimum
								                 TWE (Essay) score of 4.0 or TOEFL IBT score of 88 with no section below 20).
								Corequisite(s): None.
								Pre- or corequisite(s): None.

								                 minimum score of 30/40 or level 5 in the essay section, or (TOEFL [Test of

								                 no section below 20).

English for Academic Purposes

EAP 074  	READING & WRITING ADVANCED  6.0
								Course description: https://www.ufv.ca/calendar/current/CourseDescriptions/EAP.htm#074
								Prerequisite(s): One of EAP 064, placement by department assessment, or one of the following
								                 test scores: IELTS (Academic) score of 5.5 or higher with a minimum band score
								                 of 5.0, TOEFL iBT score of 71 or higher with no section below 16, EIKEN Grade
								                 2A with CSE total score 2400 or with Stage 1 total score result 65, PTE score
								                 of 46 with 44 in writing, LPI essay score of 18 (level 3), or CLBPT/CLBPA 7
								                 overall with no skill less than 7. Other assessments may be used to demonstrate
								                 English proficiency. Please contact upgrading@ufv.ca if a different assessment
								                 has been completed.
								Corequisite(s): None.
								Pre- or corequisite(s): None.

HIST 318  	THEMES IN MODERN GERMAN HIST.  4.0
								Course description: https://www.ufv.ca/calendar/current/CourseDescriptions/HIST.htm#318
								Prerequisite(s): Nine credits of lower-level history or 45 university-level credits.

MATH 270  	INTRO TO PROBABILITY AND STATS  4.0
								Course description: https://www.ufv.ca/calendar/current/CourseDescriptions/MATH.htm#270
								Prerequisite(s): One of the following: MATH 112, MATH 118, or a B or better in MATH 141.
								Corequisite(s): None.
								Pre- or corequisite(s): None.

	90826 AB1 Shaun Sun                             ABD    139    T R   0830  0950  09-SEP-2026 09-DEC-2026        MAX SEATS: 36
	                                                ABD    242      R   1000  1120  09-SEP-2026 09-DEC-2026        RESERVED SEATS: 0
	Instructional Method: Face-to-Face Learning (TRD)
	NOTE: This course is offered as STAT 270 and MATH 270. Students may only take one of
	      these for credit.
	NOTE: As a general rule, students with Mathematics 11 are prepared to take STAT 104,
	      those with Mathematics 12 are prepared to take STAT 106, and those with a full
	      year of calculus are prepared to take STAT/MATH 270. Before registering, students
	      should check the requirements of their program. The Mathematics major program
	      requires STAT/MATH 270, while the Mathematics minor program requires STAT 106 or
	      STAT/MATH 270.

								Corequisite(s): None.
								Pre- or corequisite(s): SOWK 404 and SOWK 410. Note: Students in the Child Welfare
								                        specialization must have the following additional pre/corequisites: SOWK
								                        412, SOWK 483, and SOWK 491.

	91049 PR1 Rita Dhungel                                                          09-SEP-2026 09-DEC-2026        MAX SEATS: 16
	                                                ONLINE UFV   M      1730  2020  21-SEP-2026 21-SEP-2026        RESERVED SEATS: 0
	                                                ONLINE UFV   M      1730  2020  05-OCT-2026 05-OCT-2026 
	                                                ONLINE UFV   M      1730  2020  19-OCT-2026 19-OCT-2026 
	                                                ONLINE UFV   M      1730  2020  02-NOV-2026 02-NOV-2026 
	                                                ONLINE UFV   M      1730  2020  16-NOV-2026 16-NOV-2026 
	                                                ONLINE UFV   M      1730  2020  30-NOV-2026 30-NOV-2026 
	Instructional Method: Online w/meeting times (OLM)
	Attributes: Zero Textbook Cost ; Upper level course ; Work Integrated Learning
	NOTE: Attendance at practicum placement and seminars is required. Students must be
	      available to complete 420 hours at the designated practicum site during its
	      operating hours. Students are expected to complete 28 hours per week for 13 to 15
	      weeks, or 14 hours per week over 26 to 30 weeks in the A/B option.
	NOTE: This course is offered either as SOWK 430 or SOWK 430A and 430B. When run as SOWK
	      430A and SOWK 430B, the course takes place over two terms in the same academic
	      year. Students must successfully complete SOWK 430A and SOWK 430B to receive
	      credit.
	NOTE: Students are encouraged to take this course in the final semester of their
	      program. It may be taken alone or alongside up to two other courses
	NOTE: School permission is required prior to registration.
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Statistics

STAT 104  	INTRODUCTORY STATISTICS  4.0
								Course description: https://www.ufv.ca/calendar/current/CourseDescriptions/STAT.htm#104
								Prerequisite(s): One of the following: (One of the following: (C or better in one of Principles
								                 of Mathematics 11, Applications of Mathematics 11, MATH 085, Foundations of
								                 Mathematics 11, Pre-calculus 11, Calculus 12, or Statistics 12) or (B or better
								                 in one of Workplace Mathematics 11, History of Mathematics 11, Apprenticeship
								                 Mathematics 12, or Apprenticeship and Workplace Mathematics 12) or (one of
								                 Foundations of Mathematics 12, Pre-calculus 12, Principles of Mathematics 12,
								                 or Applications of Mathematics 12) or (any UFV MATH course numbered 092 or
								                 higher) or (a score of 17/25 or better on Part A of the MSAT) or (45
								                 university-level credits with department permission).
								Corequisite(s): None.
								Pre- or corequisite(s): None.

	91067 AB1 Gillian Mimmack                       ABD    223    T     1000  1120  09-SEP-2026 09-DEC-2026        MAX SEATS: 36
	                                                ABA    421      RF  1000  1120  09-SEP-2026 09-DEC-2026        RESERVED SEATS: 0
	Instructional Method: Face-to-Face Learning (TRD)
	NOTE: As a general rule, students with Mathematics 11 are prepared to take STAT 104,
	      those with Mathematics 12 are prepared to take STAT 106, and those with a full
	      year of calculus are prepared to take STAT 270/MATH 270. Before registering,
	      students should check the requirements of their program. The UFV Mathematics major
	      program requires STAT 270, while the Mathematics minor program requires STAT 106
	      or STAT/MATH 270.
	NOTE: Some degree and diploma credentials may allow only one of STAT 104 or STAT 106 to
	      count as credit towards meeting program requirements.

	91068 AB2 Ken Li                                ABD    217    T     1730  1920  09-SEP-2026 09-DEC-2026        MAX SEATS: 36
	                                                ABD    226      R   1730  1920  09-SEP-2026 09-DEC-2026        RESERVED SEATS: 0

	      those with Mathematics 12 are prepared to take STAT 106, and those with a full
	      year of calculus are prepared to take STAT 270/MATH 270. Before registering,
	      students should check the requirements of their program. The UFV Mathematics major
	      program requires STAT 270, while the Mathematics minor program requires STAT 106
	      or STAT/MATH 270.
	NOTE: Some degree and diploma credentials may allow only one of STAT 104 or STAT 106 to
	      count as credit towards meeting program requirements.

	                                                ABD    242       F  1000  1120  09-SEP-2026 09-DEC-2026        RESERVED SEATS: 0

	      those with Mathematics 12 are prepared to take STAT 106, and those with a full
	      year of calculus are prepared to take STAT 270/MATH 270. Before registering,
	      students should check the requirements of their program. The UFV Mathematics major
	      program requires STAT 270, while the Mathematics minor program requires STAT 106
	      or STAT/MATH 270.
	NOTE: Some degree and diploma credentials may allow only one of STAT 104 or STAT 106 to
	      count as credit towards meeting program requirements.

	                                                                                                               RESERVED SEATS: 0

THEA 199  	INTRO TO TECHNICAL PRODUCTION  3.0
								Course description: https://www.ufv.ca/calendar/current/CourseDescriptions/THEA.htm#199
								Prerequisite(s): THEA 121 or THEA 123.
								Corequisite(s): None.
								Pre- or corequisite(s): None.

	91094 AB1 Jared Raschke                         ABD    115      R   1130  1420  09-SEP-2026 09-DEC-2026        MAX SEATS: 15
	                                                                                                               RESERVED SEATS: 0
	Instructional Method: Face-to-Face Learning (TRD)
	Attributes: Zero Textbook Cost ; Work Integrated Learning
	NOTE: Production: 1hr Adaptation of Shakespeare's The Tempest. Production dates: Nov.
	      26-28 and Dec 3-5. In addition to the regularly scheduled class time, students
	      working on design or backstage roles will be required to attend build calls and
	      meetings outside of normal class time during the rehearsal process, and will be
	      required to attend technical calls and dress rehearsals from the week of October
	      13th to the end of term on Tuesdays 11:30-14:20. Performances will take place Nov
	      26-28 and Dec 3-5. Depending on their role in the production, students may be
	      required to arrive up to 2 hours before each scheduled performance or attend other
	      scheduled work sessions during the run of the show. The full production schedule
	      will be available to students at least one month before term start. Contact
	      giuseppe.condello@ufv.ca for access to the schedule.
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
THEA 209C 	ENVIRONMENTAL THEATRE  3.0
								Course description: https://www.ufv.ca/calendar/current/CourseDescriptions/THEA.htm#209C
								Prerequisite(s): 15 university-level credits.
								Corequisite(s): None.
								Pre- or corequisite(s): None.

	91276 AB1 Parjad Sharifi                        ABD    104   M      1130  1420  09-SEP-2026 09-DEC-2026        MAX SEATS: 24
	                                                                                                               RESERVED SEATS: 0
	Instructional Method: Face-to-Face Learning (TRD)
	NOTE: This course will be offered under different letter designations (e.g. C-Z)
	      representing different topics and may be repeated for credit provided the letter
	      designation differs.
	NOTE: Integrating principles of Theatre Ecology, this course considers how performance
	      is shaped by environmental, social, and material systems. It introduces the
	      environmental theatre movement from the 1960's to the present, through
	      site-specific and immersive approaches. Students will transform classrooms,
	      outdoor locations, or found spaces into dynamic theatrical environments while
	      exploring ecological responsibility and audience interaction.
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
THEA 250  	STORYTELLING  3.0
								Course description: https://www.ufv.ca/calendar/current/CourseDescriptions/THEA.htm#250
								Prerequisite(s): None.
								Corequisite(s): None
								Pre- or corequisite(s): None

	Instructional Method: Face-to-Face Learning (TRD)
	NOTE: This course may include field trips outside of class time.

								Course description: https://www.ufv.ca/calendar/current/CourseDescriptions/THEA.htm#290

VA 110  	CREATING ON A THEME  3.0
								Course description: https://www.ufv.ca/calendar/current/CourseDescriptions/VA.htm#110
								Prerequisite(s): None.
								Corequisite(s): None.

	91104 AB1 Melanie Jones                         ABC    1031  M      0830  1120  09-SEP-2026 09-DEC-2026        MAX SEATS: 25
	                                                                                                               RESERVED SEATS: 0
	Instructional Method: Face-to-Face Learning (TRD)
	Attributes: Zero Textbook Cost
	NOTE: Theme: Monsters. This themed course introduces Monster Theory and explores
	      Monsters as cultural symbols and storytelling tools. Both 2D & 3D mediums will be
	      introduced.

	91105 AB2 Twyla Exner                           ABC    1031    W    0830  1120  09-SEP-2026 09-DEC-2026        MAX SEATS: 25
	                                                                                                               RESERVED SEATS: 0
	Instructional Method: Face-to-Face Learning (TRD)
	Attributes: Zero Textbook Cost
	NOTE: Theme: Trash to Transformation - In this hands-on studio course, students
	      transform discarded and everyday materials into imaginative artworks that respond
	      to environmental and social issues.

	91106 ON1 Aimee Brown                           ONLINE UFV                      09-SEP-2026 09-DEC-2026        MAX SEATS: 25
	                                                                                                               RESERVED SEATS: 0
	Instructional Method: Online-No meeting times (OLO)
	Attributes: Zero Textbook Cost
	NOTE: VA 110: Creating on a Theme: WORLDBUILDING Create elements from imaginary worlds
	      constructed through online class exercises. Your invented geographics, cultures
	      and creatures will inform 2D and 3D fundamental visual art skill-building.
